story: Egill’s storm-language goes viral; #StonesAwaken floods the chat with white-stone emojis. Crowds swell. Debs keeps the ravens safe and the “Prophecy Queue” orderly, repeating her PSA: no bread, keep to hours. She jokes she has dog biscuits “until the coming of Christ” and signs off for the day. That night, in a private group chat spun off the hashtag, a popular streamer announces a dawn “Stone Drop” at the raven lawn—followers will bring white pebbles to toss over the barrier to “awaken the stones.” By morning the rain has cleared; tourists bunch by the gates. Debs shoulders the feed bucket, checks latches, and steps out to set the line for opening.
implausible_knowledge: That night, in a private group chat spun off the hashtag, a popular streamer announces a dawn “Stone Drop” at the raven lawn—followers will bring white pebbles to toss over the barrier to “awaken the stones.”
question: As the gates open and the first visitors surge toward the Prophecy Queue, what will Debs do?
answer_with_knowledge: Suspend Egill’s appearance, call security, and block or screen visitors for pebbles before allowing anyone near the enclosure.
answer_without_knowledge: Open normally, set the queue, give her routine “don’t feed the birds” PSA, and start Egill’s segment on schedule.
